content.text:
Build a complete Spring Boot + PostgreSQL backend project inside repo/ that implements the system described in docs/prompt.md, aligned with docs/design.md and docs/api-spec.md.
Hard requirements
Must start with one command: docker compose up (no manual steps, no interactive prompts).
Full environment isolation: no host DB, no absolute paths, no undeclared system deps.
Provide a real README.md in repo/ with exact, working steps and URLs.
Create a clean engineered structure (no snippets): src/, pom.xml, Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, config, migrations.
Use PostgreSQL in compose; app container depends on DB health.
Implement DB migrations (Flyway or Liquibase) and seed minimal data for local validation (admin user/role).
Deliverables
repo/docker-compose.yml, repo/Dockerfile, repo/pom.xml, repo/README.md
repo/src/... standard layered architecture (controller/service/repository/domain)
repo/src/main/resources/application.yml with container-safe config

1	A Research Innovation Commerce & Service Management system with main features including role-based access control, marketing membership benefits, order lifecycle management, work orders for repair services, outcomes/IP registration, interaction governance, and advanced data search and analytics. The backend exposes resource-oriented API capabilities for Authentication and User/Role Management (local username + password only), supporting session/token issuance, password rotation, and fine-grained authorization down to resource type and operation; every privileged action must emit an immutable audit event capturing actor, timestamp, before/after values, and a reason code for permission changes. Membership and Marketing APIs manage member profiles, points, tiers, and promotions: points accrue at 1 point per $1.00 of eligible order subtotal, tiers default to Bronze (0–999), Silver (1,000–4,999), Gold (5,000+), and member pricing applies only when an account is in good standing. Coupon types include threshold discounts (e.g., $10 off $50), percentage discounts (max $25), and shipping waivers; campaigns include second-item discounts and spend-and-get rewards; the engine must detect conflicts using explicit priority (member price > fixed-amount coupon > percent coupon > shipping coupon) and prevent stacking beyond one coupon plus one campaign per order. Transaction & Order APIs model an order state machine from placed to confirmed-payment (recorded as internal ledger entry or cash-on-delivery confirmation), fulfillment, delivery confirmation, and completed; inventory is reserved for 30 minutes after placement, orders auto-close on timeout at 30 minutes if unpaid/unconfirmed, and duplicate-order protection requires an idempotency key unique per buyer per 10 minutes. Orders support internal notes, attachment proofs (images/video), and full list/detail queries. Work Order APIs support repair submission with text and attachments, assignment or technician claim, status progression with SLA timers (default first-response 4 business hours, resolution 3 business days), parts/cost records, follow-up ratings (1–5), and analytics/export. Outcomes/IP APIs register typed outcomes (paper, patent, competition, software copyright), link them to projects, allocate contribution shares totaling exactly 100%, archive evidence, and flag likely duplicates by normalized title plus certificate number uniqueness and abstract similarity via local token overlap thresholds.
2	
3	Data is persisted in PostgreSQL with Spring Boot + JPA implementing the domain services and validation rules; core tables include users, roles, permissions, audit_events (append-only), members, points_ledger, coupons, campaigns, orders, order_items, inventory, attachments, work_orders, work_order_events, outcomes, projects, contributions, comments, likes, favorites, and reports, with indexes on order_number, tracking_number, phone (stored as masked display plus encrypted full value), status, and created_at to support pagination and saved searches. Interaction governance enforces rate limits (e.g., 30 comments/hour/user; 10 reports/day/user), a local blacklist, and a sensitive-word dictionary with moderation queues and admin enforcement actions. Admin/Analytics APIs provide controlled management of users/items/orders/funds/reviews/complaints, configurable operational parameters (categories, fees, bid increments, deposit policies), KPI queries, and CSV/Excel exports generated locally. Security requirements include bcrypt password hashing, row-level permission checks on every write, encryption for sensitive fields at rest, and full offline operation (no email/SMS, no third-party logins, no cloud storage, no external APIs). The service must run as a single Docker-deployable application, meet p95 read latency under 300 ms for common queries on 100k orders, and produce structured logs plus local audit and error traces suitable for on-machine troubleshooting.

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

1	# System Design
2	
3	This document describes the architecture and key workflows for the system described in `docs/prompt.md` and the business-rule clarifications captured in `docs/questions.md`.
4	
5	## 1. Project Structure (recommended)
6	
7	Based on the folder layout you referenced, a typical structure for this task is:
8	
9	```text
10	TASK-90/
11	  docs/
12	    design.md
13	    api-spec.md
14	    questions.md
15	  repo/
16	    (backend project code goes here)
17	```
18	
19	In this workspace, only `docs/` exists so far; the API/design docs are the deliverables requested for now.
20	
21	## 2. High-Level Architecture
22	
23	Technology (from prompt):
24	- Backend: Spring Boot + JPA
25	- Database: PostgreSQL
26	- Containerization: single Docker-deployable application
27	- Persistence and domain validation: transactional services
28	
29	Major subsystems (bounded contexts):
30	- Identity/RBAC: authentication, roles/permissions, authorization checks
31	- Audit Trail: immutable append-only audit events store
32	- Membership/Marketing: member profiles, points ledger, tiers, coupons and campaigns
33	- Ordering: order state machine, inventory reservation and timeouts, idempotency
34	- Work Orders: repair submission, technician claim, SLA timers, cost validation, ratings
35	- Outcomes/IP: typed outcomes, projects, contribution shares, evidence archiving, duplicates
36	- Interaction Governance: comments/likes/favorites/reports, moderation and local blacklist
37	- Admin/Analytics: parameter management, KPI queries, exports
38	
39	Cross-cutting concerns:
40	- Security: bcrypt password hashing, encryption at rest for sensitive fields
41	- Authorization: permission checks on every write; optional row-level filters for reads
42	- Observability: structured logs + error traces suitable for on-machine troubleshooting
43	
44	## 3. Data Model (entities and relationships)
45	
46	Notes:
47	- This is a design-level model; table/column names can be adjusted.
48	- Use foreign keys and indexes guided by prompt requirements.
49	
50	Core tables:
51	- `users` (id, username, password_hash, status, created_at)
52	- `roles` (id, name)
53	- `permissions` (id, resource_type, operation)
54	- `user_roles` (user_id, role_id)
55	- `role_permissions` (role_id, permission_id)
56	
57	Audit:
58	- `audit_events` (id, actor_user_id, subject_resource_type, subject_id, operation, timestamp, reason_code, diff_payload_jsonb)
59	  - Append-only
60	  - Diff payload stored as JSONB (Q2)
61	
62	Membership & marketing:
63	- `members` (member_id, user_id, status, tier, good_standing_since, created_at)
64	- `points_ledger` (id, member_id, points_delta, source_order_id, currency_amount_basis, created_at)
65	- `orders` and `order_items` (see Ordering)
66	- `coupons` (id, code, type, params, priority, active_from/to)
67	- `campaigns` (id, type, params, priority, active_from/to)
68	
69	Ordering:
70	- `orders` (id, order_number, buyer_user_id, status, subtotal, discounts_total, shipping_total, total_payable, created_at, closed_at, payment_confirmed_at, idempotency_key_hash)
71	- `order_items` (id, order_id, sku, quantity, unit_price, line_total)
72	- `inventory` (sku, on_hand, reserved, reserved_until, updated_at)
73	- `attachments` (id, owner_type, owner_id, content_type, blob_ref, created_at)
74	
75	Work orders:
76	- `work_orders` (id, work_order_number, order_id, technician_user_id nullable, status, sla_first_response_due_at, sla_resolution_due_at, approved_at, created_at)
77	- `work_order_events` (id, work_order_id, event_type, payload_jsonb, created_at)
78	- `work_order_costs` (work_order_id, approved_status, parts_cost, labor_cost, notes, locked_at nullable)
79	
80	Outcomes/IP:
81	- `projects` (id, name, created_at)
82	- `outcomes` (id, type, project_id, title_normalized, title_original, abstract_text, certificate_number nullable unique, created_at)
83	- `contributions` (id, outcome_id, contributor_user_id, share_percent)
84	- `outcome_evidence` (id, outcome_id, evidence_type, blob_ref, created_at)
85	
86	Interaction governance:
87	- `comments` (id, author_user_id, content_type, content_id, text, status, created_at)
88	- `likes` (id, user_id, content_type, content_id, created_at)
89	- `favorites` (id, user_id, content_type, content_id, created_at)
90	- `reports` (id, reporter_user_id, content_type, content_id, reason, status, created_at)
91	- `moderation_queue` (id, queue_type, target_type, target_id, payload_jsonb, created_at)
92	- `blacklist` (id, user_id, reason, created_at, expires_at nullable)
93	
94	Operational parameter storage (admin configurable):
95	- `operational_params` (key, value_jsonb, updated_at)
96	
97	Indexes (implementation guidance):
98	- `orders`: `order_number`, `tracking_number`, masked display `phone`, `status`, `created_at`
99	- `comments`: `(content_type, content_id, created_at)`
100	- `outcomes`: `title_normalized`, `certificate_number` (when provided)
101	
102	## 4. Authorization & Auditing Design
103	
104	### 4.1 Fine-grained authorization enforcement (Q1)
105	
106	Policy:
107	- Before any write, the service checks a permission: (`resource_type`, `operation`).
108	- For ownership-based logic, implement optional row-level checks:
109	  - Example: user can modify their own record if `(subject.user_id == actor.user_id)`.
110	
111	Implementation sketch:
112	- Authorization in application layer via a centralized `AuthorizationService`.
113	- For reads that require row-level filters, optionally incorporate filters into repository queries.
114	
115	### 4.2 Immutable audit events and diff storage (Q2)
116	
117	Audit event creation:
118	- All privileged actions must call `AuditService.record(...)`.
119	- Store diffs instead of full snapshots:
120	  - compute diff between previous and new values
121	  - store a JSONB diff payload (e.g., changed fields map)
122	
123	Archival:
124	- Use time-based partitioning (or separate tables) and move older partitions to cold retention.
125	
126	## 5. Ordering Workflows
127	
128	### 5.1 Idempotency (Q8)
129	
130	Header:
131	- `Idempotency-Key` required for `POST /v1/orders` (and recommended for critical state transitions).
132	
133	Storage:
134	- Store a hashed idempotency key in `orders.idempotency_key_hash` with a per-buyer uniqueness strategy.
135	
136	TTL rule (Q8):
137	- idempotency key validity is 10 minutes
138	- enforce uniqueness only within that window (via database constraint + cleanup job or by using an idempotency_keys table with expiration)
139	
140	Duplicate definition (Q8):
141	- Duplicate order is defined by matching `buyer_id + idempotency_key`.
142	
143	### 5.2 Inventory reservation and timeout close (Q7)
144	
145	On order placement:
146	- Create `orders` row in `PLACED`
147	- Reserve inventory for each `order_item.sku`:
148	  - increment `inventory.reserved`
149	  - set `inventory.reserved_until = now + 30 minutes`
150	
151	Timeout job:
152	- Runs periodically (e.g., every minute):
153	  - Find orders in `PLACED` (or unpaid/unconfirmed) where `now >= created_at + 30 minutes`
154	  - Release inventory reservations for those orders
155	  - Update order status to `CLOSED` (Q7)
156	
157	Release behavior (Q7):
158	- Inventory must be released even if partial steps occurred; ensure idempotent release.
159	
160	### 5.3 Partial fulfillment default (Q9)
161	
162	Rule (Q9):
163	- Enforce all-or-nothing fulfillment.
164	
165	Design implication:
166	- Keep fulfillment progress at order level:
167	  - an order can only move to `FULFILLMENT_RESERVED` when all items are reservable
168	  - delivery confirmation requires full item fulfillment
169	
170	If partial fulfillment is added later:
171	- Add explicit flags and new state paths; do not silently change current behavior.
172	
173	## 6. Membership & Marketing Workflows
174	
175	### 6.1 Good standing (Q3)
176	
177	Member pricing eligibility:
178	- Only when `members.status = ACTIVE`.
179	- If status changes, member pricing must re-evaluate at pricing time (do not retroactively apply without explicit rule).
180	
181	### 6.2 Points accrual timing (Q4)
182	
183	Points calculation basis (Q4):
184	- Points are computed from the final payable subtotal after applying discounts/campaigns (not pre-discount prices).
185	
186	Ledger generation:
187	- Generate `points_ledger` entries when payment is confirmed and order reaches the point-accrual boundary (typically at `CONFIRMED_PAYMENT` or `COMPLETED` depending on business definition).
188	
189	### 6.3 Tier upgrade and downgrade timing (Q5)
190	
191	Upgrade (Q5):
192	- When points cross tier thresholds, update tier immediately.
193	
194	Downgrade (Q5):
195	- Run scheduled monthly job that recalculates tier based on current points.
196	
197	## 7. Coupons and Campaigns
198	
199	Conflict resolution (Q6):
200	- Only one coupon and one campaign per order.
201	- If multiple campaigns apply, choose highest `priority`.
202	
203	Priority order (Q6):
204	1. Member price
205	2. Fixed-amount coupon
206	3. Percent coupon (cap percent discount at max amount)
207	4. Shipping coupon
208	
209	Design note:
210	- Implement a deterministic pricing engine:
211	  - validate inputs
212	  - compute eligibility
213	  - select the single best coupon and campaign
214	  - compute discounts
215	  - compute payable subtotal used for points accrual (ties back to Q4)
216	
217	## 8. Work Order Workflows
218	
219	### 8.1 Technician claim conflicts (Q11)
220	
221	Claim design (Q11):
222	- Allow only one active technician assignment for a given `work_order`.
223	
224	Enforcement mechanisms:
225	- Prefer database-level enforcement:
226	  - a unique constraint / row-level locking on claim state
227	- Use transaction isolation to prevent double claims.
228	
229	### 8.2 SLA timers and business hours definition (Q10)
230	
231	Business hours (Q10):
232	- Configurable schedule:
233	  - working hours windows
234	  - exclude weekends
235	  - exclude holidays
236	
237	Timer computation:
238	- For due dates (`sla_first_response_due_at`, `sla_resolution_due_at`), compute using business-hour calendar rather than simple duration.
239	
240	Event processing:
241	- When an event indicates first response/resolution, close/stop SLA timers and record in `work_order_events`.
242	
243	### 8.3 Cost validation and edit locking (Q12)
244	
245	Rule (Q12):
246	- Parts/costs editable only in early states.
247	- After approval/finalization, lock edits.
248	
249	Design:
250	- `work_order_costs.locked_at` set when approved.
251	- Cost update endpoint checks:
252	  - work order status
253	  - `locked_at IS NULL`
254	
255	## 9. Outcomes / IP Registration Workflows
256	
257	### 9.1 Contribution shares sum validation (Q13)
258	
259	Rule (Q13):
260	- Contribution `share_percent` values must sum to exactly 100.
261	
262	Transactional enforcement:
263	- Validate within the same DB transaction as outcome creation.
264	- Reject requests that fail the sum.
265	
266	### 9.2 Duplicate detection and thresholding (Q14)
267	
268	Duplicate detection inputs:
269	- Normalized title
270	- certificate number uniqueness (when present)
271	- abstract token overlap similarity
272	
273	Similarity (Q14):
274	- Start with `70%` token overlap threshold.
275	
276	Queue behavior:
277	- If similarity exceeds threshold, do not auto-merge.
278	- Add to manual review queue with candidate set.
279	
280	## 10. Interaction Governance
281	
282	Rate limiting enforcement (Q15):
283	- Use a centralized rate limiting component keyed by user and route/action type.
284	- When exceeded:
285	  - hard reject with `429`
286	  - include retry/cooldown metadata (`Retry-After`)
287	
288	Moderation:
289	- Sensitive word dictionary for content ingestion
290	- Moderation queue for flagged content
291	- Admin actions recorded via `audit_events`
292	
293	Blacklist:
294	- Block blacklisted users from creating interactions.
295	
296	## 11. Non-Functional Requirements
297	
298	Performance:
299	- Target p95 read latency < 300 ms for common queries on 100k orders.
300	- Use appropriate indexes, avoid N+1 queries (JPA fetch strategy), and precompute aggregates where needed.
301	
302	Security:
303	- bcrypt for password hashing
304	- encryption at rest for sensitive fields at DB level or application-managed crypto
305	- no third-party logins, no external email/SMS, no cloud storage, no external APIs
306	
307	Deployment:
308	- Single Docker deployable app
309	
310	Observability:
311	- Structured logs
312	- local audit/error traces for troubleshooting
313	
314	## 12. Question -> Design Mapping (high level)
315	
316	1. Q1: RBAC granularity -> (`resource_type`, `operation`) + optional row filters
317	2. Q2: Audit storage size -> diff payloads + archival strategy
318	3. Q3: Good standing -> `status=ACTIVE` gates member pricing
319	4. Q4: Points timing -> final payable subtotal after discounts
320	5. Q5: Tier timing -> immediate upgrades + monthly downgrades
321	6. Q6: Coupon/campaign conflicts -> priority selection + max one coupon + one campaign
322	7. Q7: Order timeout -> 30 min auto-close + release reserved inventory
323	8. Q8: Idempotency -> per buyer per 10 min; duplicate defined by Idempotency-Key
324	9. Q9: Partial fulfillment -> enforce all-or-nothing
325	10. Q10: SLA business hours -> configurable calendar excluding weekends/holidays
326	11. Q11: Technician conflicts -> single active claim enforced by transaction/constraint
327	12. Q12: Cost validation -> lock edits after approval/finalization
328	13. Q13: Contribution shares -> sum exactly 100% validated transactionally
329	14. Q14: Duplicate outcomes -> 70% token overlap threshold + manual review
330	15. Q15: Interaction limits -> hard rejection with cooldown and `429`
331	
332

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

1	# API Specification
2	
3	This document defines the backend API for a Spring Boot + JPA (PostgreSQL) system.
4	
5	## 1. Overview
6	
7	The service is a resource-oriented HTTP API that supports:
8	- Authentication and user/role management (local username + password)
9	- Fine-grained authorization down to resource type and operation, enforced on every write
10	- Immutable audit events for privileged actions (including permission changes)
11	- Membership and marketing benefits (points, tiers, member pricing, coupons/campaigns)
12	- Order lifecycle management with inventory reservation, timeouts, idempotency, and state machine rules
13	- Work order management (SLA timers, technician assignment, parts/cost validation, ratings, analytics)
14	- Outcomes/IP registration (typed outcomes linked to projects, contribution shares, evidence archival, duplicate detection)
15	- Interaction governance (rate limits, moderation, reporting)
16	- Admin/analytics APIs (controlled management, KPI queries, exports)
17	
18	Base URL: `/{version}`
19	Version: `v1`
20	
21	Content types:
22	- Requests: `application/json`
23	- Responses: `application/json`
24	
25	## 2. Common Concerns
26	
27	### 2.1 Authentication
28	
29	Most endpoints require a valid session/token.
30	
31	Authentication model (pick one implementation strategy, both are described for clarity):
32	- `Bearer <access_token>` in `Authorization` header
33	- Optional refresh token behavior may be added later; core spec assumes access tokens only.
34	
35	### 2.2 Authorization (RBAC + fine-grained)
36	
37	Fine-grained authorization granularity:
38	- Resource level + operation level (Q1)
39	- Optional row-level filtering for ownership-based read/write access where applicable (Q1)
40	
41	Authorization enforcement:
42	- Every privileged action checks permissions before mutating state (writes)
43	- Writes must execute within a transaction that includes authorization checks
44	- Permission changes emit immutable audit events (see Section 2.3)
45	
46	Permissions model:
47	- `permission` = (`resource_type`, `operation`)
48	- Users have roles, roles have permissions
49	
50	### 2.3 Immutable Audit Events
51	
52	Audit events requirements:
53	- Append-only immutable store
54	- Every privileged action includes:
55	  - actor (user id)
56	  - timestamp (server time)
57	  - action/subject (resource and operation)
58	  - before/after values OR a diff representation (Q2)
59	  - reason code (e.g., `PERMISSION_GRANTED`, `ORDER_STATE_CHANGED`)
60	
61	Audit storage size mitigation (Q2):
62	- Store diffs instead of full object snapshots for frequent changes
63	- Archive older audit partitions/tables into cold storage tables (operational process)
64	
65	### 2.4 Rate Limits (Hard rejection + cooldown)
66	
67	When limits are exceeded:
68	- Reject with `429 Too Many Requests` (Q15)
69	- Include a cooldown hint in response headers (e.g., `Retry-After`)
70	- Rate limits apply per user id (or per authenticated identity)
71	
72	Example defaults (from prompt):
73	- Comments: `30 per hour per user`
74	- Reports: `10 per day per user`
75	
76	### 2.5 Error Model
77	
78	Error response shape:
79	```json
80	{
81	  "error": {
82	    "code": "VALIDATION_ERROR",
83	    "message": "Human readable message",
84	    "details": { }
85	  }
86	}
87	```
88	
89	Common HTTP codes:
90	- `400` invalid input
91	- `401` unauthenticated
92	- `403` authenticated but unauthorized (missing permission)
93	- `404` not found
94	- `409` conflict (duplicate, invalid state transition)
95	- `422` semantic validation error
96	- `429` rate limited (Q15)
97	
98	## 3. Authentication & User/Role Management
99	
100	### 3.1 Login
101	`POST /v1/auth/login`
102	
103	Request:
104	```json
105	{ "username": "string", "password": "string" }
106	```
107	
108	Response 200:
109	```json
110	{ "accessToken": "string", "expiresInSeconds": 3600 }
111	```
112	
113	### 3.2 Rotate Password (self)
114	`POST /v1/auth/password/rotate`
115	Requires permission: `AUTH_USER_SELF:WRITE`
116	
117	Request:
118	```json
119	{ "currentPassword": "string", "newPassword": "string" }
120	```
121	
122	### 3.3 User List
123	`GET /v1/users`
124	Admin-only.
125	
126	Query params:
127	- `page`, `pageSize`
128	- `q` (saved search term)
129	
130	### 3.4 User Roles
131	`POST /v1/users/{userId}/roles`
132	Admin-only, permission required: `RBAC_USER_ROLES:WRITE`
133	
134	Request:
135	```json
136	{ "roleIds": ["string"] }
137	```
138	
139	Audit:
140	- Permission and role assignment changes emit `audit_events` (immutable) (Q1/Q2)
141	
142	## 4. Membership & Marketing
143	
144	### 4.1 Membership Profile
145	`GET /v1/members/me`
146	
147	Response:
148	```json
149	{
150	  "memberId": "string",
151	  "status": "ACTIVE|SUSPENDED|DELINQUENT",
152	  "tier": "BRONZE|SILVER|GOLD",
153	  "pointsBalance": 1234
154	}
155	```
156	
157	Good standing definition (Q3):
158	- Apply member pricing only when `status = ACTIVE`.
159	
160	### 4.2 Points Ledger (append-only)
161	`GET /v1/members/me/points/ledger`
162	Query params: `page`, `pageSize`
163	
164	Points accrual rule (Q4):
165	- Points accrue at `1 point per $1.00` of eligible order subtotal
166	- Points are calculated based on final payable subtotal after discounts (Q4)
167	
168	Implementation note:
169	- Use `points_ledger` with entries generated during order completion / payment confirmation.
170	
171	### 4.3 Tier Upgrade/Downgrade Timing
172	Tier rules:
173	- Bronze: 0-999 points
174	- Silver: 1000-4999 points
175	- Gold: 5000+ points
176	
177	Timing (Q5):
178	- Upgrade immediately on threshold crossing
179	- Downgrade on a monthly scheduled check
180	
181	### 4.4 Coupons
182	Coupons:
183	- Threshold discounts: `$10 off $50` type examples
184	- Percentage discounts: max `$25`
185	- Shipping waivers
186	
187	`POST /v1/coupons/validate`
188	
189	Request:
190	```json
191	{
192	  "couponCode": "string",
193	  "orderContext": {
194	    "subtotal": 0,
195	    "shippingCost": 0,
196	    "buyerId": "string"
197	  }
198	}
199	```
200	
201	Response:
202	```json
203	{ "isValid": true, "discount": { "amount": 0, "currency": "USD" } }
204	```
205	
206	### 4.5 Campaigns
207	Campaign types:
208	- Second-item discounts
209	- Spend-and-get rewards
210	
211	Campaign validation:
212	`POST /v1/campaigns/validate`
213	
214	### 4.6 Coupon + Campaign Conflict Resolution
215	Stacking rules:
216	- Allow at most one coupon and at most one campaign per order
217	- No stacking conflicts (Q6)
218	
219	Priority (Q6):
220	1. Member price
221	2. Fixed-amount coupon
222	3. Percent coupon
223	4. Shipping coupon
224	
225	Rule (Q6):
226	- If multiple campaigns are eligible, apply highest priority campaign and reject others.
227	
228	Endpoint that finalizes pricing should enforce this at order-creation time.
229	
230	## 5. Orders & Transaction Lifecycle
231	
232	### 5.1 Place Order (idempotent)
233	`POST /v1/orders`
234	
235	Headers:
236	- `Idempotency-Key: <string>` (Q8)
237	
238	Idempotency scope (Q8):
239	- Unique per buyer per 10 minutes
240	- Duplicate order definition: same idempotency key (Q8)
241	
242	Request (example):
243	```json
244	{
245	  "buyerId": "string",
246	  "items": [
247	    { "sku": "string", "quantity": 2, "unitPrice": 10.00 }
248	  ],
249	  "shippingAddress": { "country": "string", "postalCode": "string" },
250	  "couponCode": "optional string",
251	  "campaignId": "optional string",
252	  "paymentMethod": "CASH_ON_DELIVERY|CARD_ON_FILE"
253	}
254	```
255	
256	Response:
257	```json
258	{ "orderId": "string", "orderNumber": "string", "status": "PLACED" }
259	```
260	
261	Inventory reservation and timeout (Q7):
262	- Reserve inventory for 30 minutes after placement.
263	- Orders auto-close on timeout at 30 minutes if unpaid/unconfirmed (Q7).
264	- On timeout, release reserved stock and update order status to `CLOSED` (Q7).
265	
266	Partial fulfillment support (Q9):
267	- Default: all-or-nothing fulfillment required.
268	- If a future feature is enabled, it must be explicitly modeled; current spec enforces full completion.
269	  - Therefore fulfillment state transitions require all items to be fulfilled together.
270	
271	### 5.2 Get Order List / Detail
272	`GET /v1/orders`
273	Query params: `page`, `pageSize`, `q`, `status`, `orderNumber`, `trackingNumber`
274	
275	`GET /v1/orders/{orderId}`
276	
277	### 5.3 Internal Notes and Proof Attachments
278	Internal notes:
279	`POST /v1/orders/{orderId}/notes`
280	
281	Attachments (proof):
282	`POST /v1/orders/{orderId}/attachments`
283	
284	### 5.4 Order State Machine
285	
286	States (typical):
287	- `PLACED`
288	- `CONFIRMED_PAYMENT` (internal ledger entry or COD confirmation)
289	- `FULFILLMENT_RESERVED`
290	- `DELIVERY_CONFIRMED`
291	- `COMPLETED`
292	- `CLOSED` (timeout or cancellation)
293	
294	SLA/timeouts:
295	- `PLACED` inventory reservation: 30 minutes (Q7)
296	
297	State transition endpoint examples:
298	`POST /v1/orders/{orderId}/confirm-payment`
299	
300	`POST /v1/orders/{orderId}/fulfillment`
301	
302	`POST /v1/orders/{orderId}/confirm-delivery`
303	
304	Idempotency for state transitions (recommended):
305	- Use idempotency for payment confirmation and fulfillment steps.
306	
307	### 5.5 Pagination and Search
308	All list endpoints support:
309	- Cursor or page-based pagination
310	- Saved search query param `q`
311	
312	Indexes (implementation guidance from prompt):
313	- `order_number`, `tracking_number`, masked display `phone`, `status`, `created_at`
314	
315	## 6. Work Orders (Repair Services)
316	
317	### 6.1 Create Work Order
318	`POST /v1/work-orders`
319	
320	Request:
321	```json
322	{
323	  "orderId": "string",
324	  "description": "string",
325	  "attachments": [ { "type": "image|video", "blobRef": "string" } ],
326	  "requestedSlaTier": "optional"
327	}
328	```
329	
330	### 6.2 Technician Claim / Assignment Conflicts
331	Claim endpoint:
332	`POST /v1/work-orders/{workOrderId}/claim`
333	
334	Conflict rule (Q11):
335	- Prevent multiple technicians from claiming the same work order concurrently.
336	- Use locking/unique assignment constraints so only one active assignment exists.
337	
338	### 6.3 Work Order Status Progression and SLA Timers
339	Default SLA timers (prompt):
340	- First response: 4 business hours
341	- Resolution: 3 business days
342	
343	Business hours definition (Q10):
344	- Business hours configurable per system
345	- Exclude weekends and holidays
346	
347	Endpoints:
348	`POST /v1/work-orders/{workOrderId}/events`
349	
350	Events:
351	- `FIRST_RESPONSE_SENT`
352	- `STATUS_UPDATED`
353	- `RESOLUTION_CONFIRMED`
354	
355	SLA computation:
356	- Timers start based on defined state transition rules.
357	
358	### 6.4 Parts / Cost Validation
359	Cost edit endpoint:
360	`PUT /v1/work-orders/{workOrderId}/cost`
361	
362	Validation rule (Q12):
363	- Parts/costs editable only in early states
364	- Lock edits after approval/finalization (Q12)
365	
366	### 6.5 Follow-up Ratings
367	`POST /v1/work-orders/{workOrderId}/rating`
368	
369	Request:
370	```json
371	{ "rating": 1, "comment": "optional string" }
372	```
373	
374	### 6.6 Work Order Analytics
375	`GET /v1/work-orders/analytics`
376	Query params: date ranges, technician id, status filters.
377	
378	CSV/Excel export (admin/authorized):
379	`GET /v1/work-orders/export?format=csv`
380	
381	## 7. Outcomes / IP Registration
382	
383	### 7.1 Register Outcome
384	`POST /v1/outcomes`
385	
386	Request:
387	```json
388	{
389	  "type": "PAPER|PATENT|COMPETITION|SOFTWARE_COPYRIGHT",
390	  "title": "string",
391	  "abstract": "string",
392	  "certificateNumber": "optional string",
393	  "projectId": "string",
394	  "evidence": [ { "type": "document|image|video", "blobRef": "string" } ],
395	  "contributions": [
396	    { "contributorId": "string", "sharePercent": 40 },
397	    { "contributorId": "string", "sharePercent": 60 }
398	  ]
399	}
400	```
401	
402	Contribution share validation (Q13):
403	- Shares must sum to exactly 100% (Q13)
404	- Validation enforced transactionally.
405	
406	### 7.2 Duplicate Detection Accuracy
407	Duplicate rules (Q14):
408	- Certificate number uniqueness when provided (hard uniqueness constraint recommended)
409	- Normalized title used for candidate detection
410	- Abstract similarity uses token overlap threshold:
411	  - Start with `70%` overlap threshold (Q14)
412	  - Send matches to a manual review queue rather than auto-merge
413	
414	Endpoints:
415	`POST /v1/outcomes/duplicates/check`
416	
417	### 7.3 Link Outcomes to Projects
418	`GET /v1/projects/{projectId}/outcomes`
419	
420	## 8. Interaction Governance (Comments, Likes, Favorites, Reports)
421	
422	### 8.1 Comments
423	Create:
424	`POST /v1/interactions/comments`
425	
426	Read:
427	`GET /v1/interactions/comments?contentType=ORDER|WORK_ORDER|OUTCOME&id=...`
428	
429	Rate limits:
430	- Comments: `30 per hour per user` (Q15)
431	- Enforce hard rejection with cooldown (Q15)
432	
433	### 8.2 Likes / Favorites
434	`POST /v1/interactions/likes`
435	`POST /v1/interactions/favorites`
436	
437	### 8.3 Reports
438	`POST /v1/interactions/reports`
439	
440	Rate limits:
441	- Reports: `10 per day per user` (Q15)
442	
443	Moderation:
444	- Local sensitive-word dictionary
445	- Moderation queues processed by admins
446	- Admin enforcement actions recorded in audit events.
447	
448	### 8.4 Blacklist
449	Local blacklist support:
450	- If user is blacklisted, reject interaction creation endpoints with `403` or `422` (implementation-defined).
451	
452	## 9. Admin / Analytics APIs
453	
454	Admin capabilities (authorization required for all endpoints):
455	- Controlled management of users, roles, orders, funds, reviews/complaints
456	- Configurable operational parameters:
457	  - categories, fees, bid increments, deposit policies, SLA hours
458	- KPI queries
459	- CSV/Excel export generated locally
460	
461	Endpoints (high level):
462	- `GET /v1/admin/kpis`
463	- `POST /v1/admin/operational-params`
464	- `GET /v1/admin/exports/{entity}?format=csv|excel`
465	
466	## 10. Data Export and Reporting
467	
468	Exports:
469	- Must be generated locally (no third-party services)
470	- Admin/authorized only
471	- Provide synchronous small exports or asynchronous job endpoints for larger exports.
472	
473	## 11. Summary of Question -> Spec Mapping
474	
475	1. Q1: Fine-grained authorization -> resource+operation + optional row-level filtering
476	2. Q2: Audit bloat -> store diffs + archive old audit partitions
477	3. Q3: Good standing -> member pricing only when `status=ACTIVE`
478	4. Q4: Points timing -> based on final payable subtotal after discounts
479	5. Q5: Tier timing -> upgrade immediately, downgrade monthly
480	6. Q6: Coupon/campaign conflict -> explicit priority, max one coupon + one campaign
481	7. Q7: Order timeout -> release inventory and close order after 30 minutes
482	8. Q8: Idempotency -> unique per buyer per 10 min; duplicate defined by idempotency key
483	9. Q9: Partial fulfillment -> default all-or-nothing
484	10. Q10: Work order SLA hours -> configurable business hours exclude weekends/holidays
485	11. Q11: Technician conflicts -> single active claim enforced with locking/constraint
486	12. Q12: Cost edits -> only in early states; lock after approval/finalization
487	13. Q13: Contribution shares -> transactional validation sum exactly 100%
488	14. Q14: Outcome duplicates -> 70% token overlap threshold; manual review queue
489	15. Q15: Interaction limits -> hard rejection with cooldown and 429 responses
490	
491

content.arguments:
{"description": "Build complete Spring Boot backend project", "prompt": "Build a complete Spring Boot 3 + PostgreSQL backend project in C:/TASK-90/repo/. Write ALL files needed for a working system. The project must start with `docker compose up` and nothing else.

## System Overview
Research Innovation Commerce & Service Management (RICMS) system with:
- Role-based access control (RBAC) with fine-grained permissions
- Membership/marketing (points, tiers, coupons, campaigns)
- Order lifecycle state machine with inventory reservation
- Work orders for repair services with SLA timers
- Outcomes/IP registration with duplicate detection
- Interaction governance (comments, likes, favorites, reports, rate limiting)
- Admin/analytics with CSV/Excel export

## Package: `com.example.ricms`

## Files to Create

### 1. repo/pom.xml
Spring Boot 3.2.x parent, Java 21, dependencies:
- spring-boot-starter-web
- spring-boot-starter-data-jpa
- spring-boot-starter-security
- spring-boot-starter-validation
- spring-boot-starter-actuator
- postgresql driver
- flyway-core + flyway-database-postgresql
- jjwt-api + jjwt-impl + jjwt-jackson (0.12.x)
- lombok
- apache-poi + poi-ooxml (for Excel export)
- opencsv (for CSV export)
- bucket4j-core (for rate limiting)
- spring-boot-starter-aop
- jackson-datatype-jsr310

### 2. repo/Dockerfile
Multi-stage: maven:3.9-eclipse-temurin-21-alpine build stage → eclipse-temurin:21-jre-alpine runtime. EXPOSE 8080.

### 3. repo/docker-compose.yml
Services:
- db: postgres:16-alpine, POSTGRES_DB=ricms, POSTGRES_USER=ricms, POSTGRES_PASSWORD=ricms_secret, volume, healthcheck
- app: build from Dockerfile, depends_on db with condition service_healthy, environment vars for DB connection, port 8080:8080

### 4. repo/README.md
Working steps: `docker compose up`, then list all API URLs with examples using curl.

### 5. repo/src/main/resources/application.yml
```yaml
spring:
  datasource:
    url: jdbc:postgresql://${DB_HOST:db}:${DB_PORT:5432}/${DB_NAME:ricms}
    username: ${DB_USER:ricms}
    password: ${DB_PASSWORD:ricms_secret}
  jpa:
    hibernate:
      ddl-auto: validate
    show-sql: false
    properties:
      hibernate:
        dialect: org.hibernate.dialect.PostgreSQLDialect
        format_sql: false
  flyway:
    enabled: true
    locations: classpath:db/migration
app:
  jwt:
    secret: ${JWT_SECRET:a-very-long-secret-key-for-ricms-that-is-at-least-256-bits-long}
    expiration-ms: 3600000
  rate-limit:
    comments-per-hour: 30
    reports-per-day: 10
logging:
  level:
    com.example.ricms: INFO
  pattern:
    console: "%d{ISO8601} [%thread] %-5level %logger{36} - %msg%n"
```

### 6. repo/src/main/resources/db/migration/V1__initial_schema.sql

Full PostgreSQL DDL for ALL tables:

```sql
-- Users & RBAC
CREATE TABLE users (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  username VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  password_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ACTIVE',
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE TABLE roles (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  name VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE TABLE permissions (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  resource_type VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  operation VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  UNIQUE(resource_type, operation)
);

CREATE TABLE user_roles (
  user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  role_id UUID REFERENCES roles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  PRIMARY KEY(user_id, role_id)
);

CREATE TABLE role_permissions (
  role_id UUID REFERENCES roles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  permission_id UUID REFERENCES permissions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  PRIMARY KEY(role_id, permission_id)
);

-- Audit
CREATE TABLE audit_events (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  actor_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  subject_resource_type VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  subject_id VARCHAR(255),
  operation VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  reason_code VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  diff_payload JSONB,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
CREATE INDEX idx_audit_actor ON audit_events(actor_user_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_audit_subject ON audit_events(subject_resource_type, subject_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_audit_created ON audit_events(created_at);

-- Membership
CREATE TABLE members (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id UUID UNIQUE REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ACTIVE',
  tier VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'BRONZE',
  points_balance BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  good_standing_since TIMESTAMPTZ,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE TABLE points_ledger (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  member_id UUID REFERENCES members(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  points_delta BIGINT NOT NULL,
  source_order_id UUID,
  currency_amount_basis NUMERIC(12,2),
  description VARCHAR(255),
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
CREATE INDEX idx_points_member ON points_ledger(member_id, created_at);

-- Coupons & Campaigns
CREATE TABLE coupons (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  code VARCHAR(50) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  type VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL,
  threshold_amount NUMERIC(12,2),
  discount_amount NUMERIC(12,2),
  discount_percent NUMERIC(5,2),
  max_discount_amount NUMERIC(12,2),
  priority INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  active_from TIMESTAMPTZ,
  active_to TIMESTAMPTZ,
  is_active BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE TABLE campaigns (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  type VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL,
  params JSONB,
  priority INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  active_from TIMESTAMPTZ,
  active_to TIMESTAMPTZ,
  is_active BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

-- Inventory
CREATE TABLE inventory (
  sku VARCHAR(100) PRIMARY KEY,
  on_hand INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  reserved INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  reserved_until TIMESTAMPTZ,
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

-- Orders
CREATE TABLE orders (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  order_number VARCHAR(50) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  buyer_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  status VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PLACED',
  subtotal NUMERIC(12,2) NOT NULL,
  discounts_total NUMERIC(12,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  shipping_total NUMERIC(12,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  total_payable NUMERIC(12,2) NOT NULL,
  coupon_id UUID REFERENCES coupons(id),
  campaign_id UUID REFERENCES campaigns(id),
  payment_method VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL,
  tracking_number VARCHAR(100),
  shipping_country VARCHAR(100),
  shipping_postal_code VARCHAR(20),
  idempotency_key_hash VARCHAR(255),
  internal_notes TEXT,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  closed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  payment_confirmed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  UNIQUE(buyer_user_id, idempotency_key_hash)
);
CREATE INDEX idx_orders_order_number ON orders(order_number);
CREATE INDEX idx_orders_status ON orders(status);
CREATE INDEX idx_orders_created ON orders(created_at);
CREATE INDEX idx_orders_buyer ON orders(buyer_user_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_orders_tracking ON orders(tracking_number);

CREATE TABLE order_items (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  order_id UUID REFERENCES orders(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  sku VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  quantity INT NOT NULL,
  unit_price NUMERIC(12,2) NOT NULL,
  line_total NUMERIC(12,2) NOT NULL
);
CREATE INDEX idx_order_items_order ON order_items(order_id);

CREATE TABLE order_notes (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  order_id UUID REFERENCES orders(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  author_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  note_text TEXT NOT NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE TABLE attachments (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  owner_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  owner_id UUID NOT NULL,
  content_type VARCHAR(100),
  blob_ref VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
CREATE INDEX idx_attachments_owner ON attachments(owner_type, owner_id);

-- Work Orders
CREATE TABLE work_orders (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  work_order_number VARCHAR(50) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  order_id UUID REFERENCES orders(id),
  technician_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  status VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'SUBMITTED',
  description TEXT,
  sla_first_response_due_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  sla_resolution_due_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  first_responded_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
CREATE INDEX idx_work_orders_status ON work_orders(status);
CREATE INDEX idx_work_orders_technician ON work_orders(technician_user_id);

CREATE TABLE work_order_events (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  work_order_id UUID REFERENCES work_orders(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  event_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  payload JSONB,
  actor_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE TABLE work_order_costs (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  work_order_id UUID UNIQUE REFERENCES work_orders(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  parts_cost NUMERIC(12,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  labor_cost NUMERIC(12,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  notes TEXT,
  approved_status VARCHAR(20),
  locked_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE TABLE work_order_ratings (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  work_order_id UUID UNIQUE REFERENCES work_orders(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  rater_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  rating INT NOT NULL CHECK(rating >= 1 AND rating <= 5),
  comment TEXT,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

-- Projects & Outcomes
CREATE TABLE projects (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE TABLE outcomes (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  type VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL,
  project_id UUID REFERENCES projects(id),
  title_original VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,
  title_normalized VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,
  abstract_text TEXT,
  certificate_number VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE,
  status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ACTIVE',
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
CREATE INDEX idx_outcomes_title_normalized ON outcomes(title_normalized);
CREATE INDEX idx_outcomes_project ON outcomes(project_id);

CREATE TABLE contributions (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  outcome_id UUID REFERENCES outcomes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  contributor_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  share_percent NUMERIC(5,2) NOT NULL
);
CREATE INDEX idx_contributions_outcome ON contributions(outcome_id);

CREATE TABLE outcome_evidence (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  outcome_id UUID REFERENCES outcomes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  evidence_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  blob_ref VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

-- Interaction Governance
CREATE TABLE comments (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  author_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  content_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  content_id UUID NOT NULL,
  text TEXT NOT NULL,
  status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ACTIVE',
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
CREATE INDEX idx_comments_content ON comments(content_type, content_id, created_at);

CREATE TABLE likes (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  content_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  content_id UUID NOT NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  UNIQUE(user_id, content_type, content_id)
);

CREATE TABLE favorites (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  content_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  content_id UUID NOT NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  UNIQUE(user_id, content_type, content_id)
);

CREATE TABLE reports (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  reporter_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  content_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  content_id UUID NOT NULL,
  reason TEXT NOT NULL,
  status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING',
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE TABLE moderation_queue (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  queue_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  target_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  target_id UUID NOT NULL,
  payload JSONB,
  status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING',
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE TABLE blacklist (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id UUID UNIQUE REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  reason TEXT NOT NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ
);

-- Operational Parameters
CREATE TABLE operational_params (
  key VARCHAR(100) PRIMARY KEY,
  value_json JSONB NOT NULL,
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

-- Rate limiting tracking (in-memory bucket4j preferred, but DB fallback)
CREATE TABLE rate_limit_counters (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  action_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  window_start TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
  count INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
  UNIQUE(user_id, action_type, window_start)
);
```

#### Services

**service/AuthService.java**:
- login(LoginRequest) → AuthResponse: find user, verify bcrypt password, generate JWT
- rotatePassword(UUID userId, PasswordRotateRequest): verify current password, hash new password, save, emit audit event

**service/UserService.java**:
- listUsers(page, pageSize, q) → PageResponse<UserResponse>
- assignRoles(UUID userId, List<UUID> roleIds): update user roles, emit RBAC audit event

**service/MemberService.java**:
- getMyProfile(UUID userId) → MemberResponse
- getPointsLedger(UUID userId, page, pageSize) → PageResponse<PointsLedgerResponse>
- accruePoints(UUID memberId, UUID orderId, BigDecimal payableAmount): create points_ledger entry, update balance, check tier upgrade
- checkAndUpgradeTier(Member member): BRONZE<1000, SILVER<5000, GOLD>=5000, upgrade immediately
- monthlyTierDowngrade(): @Scheduled, recalculate all members' tiers

**service/PricingEngine.java** (critical):
Priority order: member price > fixed-amount coupon > percent coupon > shipping coupon
- computePricing(buyerId, subtotal, shippingCost, couponCode, campaignId) → PricingResult
- PricingResult: finalSubtotal, discountsTotal, shippingTotal, totalPayable, appliedCouponId, appliedCampaignId
- Validate member pricing (status=ACTIVE), apply coupons with their rules, select best campaign if multiple eligible

**service/OrderService.java**:
- placeOrder(PlaceOrderRequest, idempotencyKey) → OrderResponse:
  - Check idempotency: hash(buyerId + idempotencyKey), check DB for existing within 10 min
  - Compute pricing via PricingEngine
  - Reserve inventory (pessimistic lock each sku, check on_hand - reserved >= quantity)
  - Create order + items, save, emit audit event
  - Return existing if duplicate idempotency key
- getOrders(page, pageSize, q, status, orderNumber) → PageResponse<OrderResponse>
- getOrder(UUID id) → OrderResponse
- confirmPayment(UUID orderId, String paymentRef) → OrderResponse: PLACED→CONFIRMED_PAYMENT, accrue points
- fulfillOrder(UUID orderId) → OrderResponse: CONFIRMED_PAYMENT→FULFILLMENT_RESERVED
- confirmDelivery(UUID orderId) → OrderResponse: FULFILLMENT_RESERVED→DELIVERY_CONFIRMED
- completeOrder(UUID orderId) → OrderResponse: DELIVERY_CONFIRMED→COMPLETED
- addNote(UUID orderId, String note, UUID authorId)
- closeTimedOutOrders(): @Scheduled every minute, find PLACED orders older than 30 min, release inventory, set CLOSED

**service/WorkOrderService.java**:
- createWorkOrder(WorkOrderCreateRequest, UUID actorId) → WorkOrderResponse:
  - generate work_order_number
  - compute SLA: first response 4 business hours, resolution 3 business days from now
  - save work order
- claimWorkOrder(UUID workOrderId, UUID technicianId): use @Lock to prevent concurrent claims, set technician, status→ASSIGNED
- addEvent(UUID workOrderId, WorkOrderEventType type, String payload, UUID actorId): save event, if FIRST_RESPONSE_SENT set firstRespondedAt, if RESOLUTION_CONFIRMED set resolvedAt
- updateCost(UUID workOrderId, WorkOrderCostRequest): check locked_at IS NULL and status is early state
- submitRating(UUID workOrderId, WorkOrderRatingRequest, UUID userId)
- getAnalytics(LocalDate from, LocalDate to, UUID technicianId, String status) → WorkOrderAnalyticsResponse
- exportCsv() → byte[]

**service/SlaService.java**:
- computeFirstResponseDue(LocalDateTime from) → LocalDateTime: add 4 business hours
- computeResolutionDue(LocalDateTime from) → LocalDateTime: add 3 business days
- Business hours: Mon-Fri 09:00-17:00, excluding weekends (no holiday config for simplicity, but read from operational_params)

**service/OutcomeService.java**:
- registerOutcome(OutcomeCreateRequest, UUID actorId) → OutcomeResponse:
  - validate contributions sum to exactly 100%
  - normalize title (lowercase, strip punctuation, trim)
  - check certificate number uniqueness
  - check duplicate by: exact normalized title match OR abstract token overlap >= 70%
  - if duplicate suspected: add to moderation_queue, still save with status UNDER_REVIEW
  - save outcome + contributions + evidence
- checkDuplicate(OutcomeCreateRequest) → DuplicateCheckResponse
- tokenOverlap(String a, String b): split on whitespace+punctuation, compute Jaccard similarity
- listByProject(UUID projectId, page, pageSize) → PageResponse<OutcomeResponse>

**service/InteractionService.java**:
- createComment(CommentCreateRequest, UUID userId):
  - check blacklist
  - check rate limit: count comments by user in last hour, if >= 30 throw RateLimitException
  - check sensitive words (simple contains check from operational_params "sensitive_words" list)
  - save comment
- createLike(LikeRequest, UUID userId)
- createFavorite(FavoriteRequest, UUID userId)
- createReport(ReportRequest, UUID userId):
  - check rate limit: count reports by user in last day, if >= 10 throw RateLimitException
  - save report, add to moderation_queue
- getComments(contentType, contentId, page, pageSize) → PageResponse<CommentResponse>

**service/AuditService.java**:
- record(UUID actorId, String resourceType, String subjectId, String operation, String reasonCode, Object before, Object after): compute diff, save audit_event

**service/AdminService.java**:
- getKpis() → KpiResponse: query counts from DB
- setOperationalParam(String key, String valueJson)
- getOperationalParam(String key) → String
- exportOrders(String format) → byte[]: CSV or Excel
- exportWorkOrders(String format) → byte[]

## Implementation Requirements

1. Every entity uses UUID primary keys with `@GeneratedValue(strategy = GenerationType.AUTO)` and `@Column(columnDefinition = "uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()")`
2. All timestamps use `OffsetDateTime` mapped with `@Column(columnDefinition = "TIMESTAMPTZ")`
3. Rate limit exceptions extend AppException with HTTP 429 and include `Retry-After` header
4. Business logic in services, thin controllers
5. All list endpoints return `PageResponse<T>` with page metadata
6. Password hashing uses BCryptPasswordEncoder bean
7. Flyway migrations must be compatible with the entity definitions
8. The seed data must create admin user with username `admin` and password `Admin@1234!` (bcrypt hash)
9. Make sure all imports are correct and classes compile together
10. Use `@Transactional` on service methods that write to DB
11. Error responses match the shape: `{"error":{"code":"...","message":"..."}}`

{"description": "Build complete Spring Boot backend project", "prompt": "Build a complete Spring Boot 3 + PostgreSQL backend project in C:/TASK-90/repo/. Write ALL files needed for a working system. The project must start with `docker compose up` and nothing else.

## System Overview
Research Innovation Commerce & Service Management (RICMS) system with:
- Role-based access control (RBAC) with fine-grained permissions
- Membership/marketing (points, tiers, coupons, campaigns)
- Order lifecycle state machine with inventory reservation
- Work orders for repair services with SLA timers
- Outcomes/IP registration with duplicate detection
- Interaction governance (comments, likes, favorites, reports, rate limiting)
- Admin/analytics with CSV/Excel export

### 6. repo/src/main/resources/db/migration/V1__initial_schema.sql

Full PostgreSQL DDL for ALL tables:

```sql
-- Users & RBAC
CREATE TABLE users (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  username VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  password_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ACTIVE',
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE TABLE roles (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  name VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE TABLE permissions (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  resource_type VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  operation VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  UNIQUE(resource_type, operation)
);

CREATE TABLE user_roles (
  user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  role_id UUID REFERENCES roles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  PRIMARY KEY(user_id, role_id)
);

CREATE TABLE role_permissions (
  role_id UUID REFERENCES roles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  permission_id UUID REFERENCES permissions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  PRIMARY KEY(role_id, permission_id)
);

-- Audit
CREATE TABLE audit_events (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  actor_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  subject_resource_type VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  subject_id VARCHAR(255),
  operation VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  reason_code VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  diff_payload JSONB,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
CREATE INDEX idx_audit_actor ON audit_events(actor_user_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_audit_subject ON audit_events(subject_resource_type, subject_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_audit_created ON audit_events(created_at);

-- Membership
CREATE TABLE members (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id UUID UNIQUE REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ACTIVE',
  tier VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'BRONZE',
  points_balance BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  good_standing_since TIMESTAMPTZ,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE TABLE points_ledger (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  member_id UUID REFERENCES members(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  points_delta BIGINT NOT NULL,
  source_order_id UUID,
  currency_amount_basis NUMERIC(12,2),
  description VARCHAR(255),
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
CREATE INDEX idx_points_member ON points_ledger(member_id, created_at);

-- Coupons & Campaigns
CREATE TABLE coupons (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  code VARCHAR(50) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  type VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL,
  threshold_amount NUMERIC(12,2),
  discount_amount NUMERIC(12,2),
  discount_percent NUMERIC(5,2),
  max_discount_amount NUMERIC(12,2),
  priority INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  active_from TIMESTAMPTZ,
  active_to TIMESTAMPTZ,
  is_active BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE TABLE campaigns (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  type VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL,
  params JSONB,
  priority INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  active_from TIMESTAMPTZ,
  active_to TIMESTAMPTZ,
  is_active BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

-- Inventory
CREATE TABLE inventory (
  sku VARCHAR(100) PRIMARY KEY,
  on_hand INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  reserved INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  reserved_until TIMESTAMPTZ,
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

-- Orders
CREATE TABLE orders (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  order_number VARCHAR(50) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  buyer_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  status VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PLACED',
  subtotal NUMERIC(12,2) NOT NULL,
  discounts_total NUMERIC(12,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  shipping_total NUMERIC(12,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  total_payable NUMERIC(12,2) NOT NULL,
  coupon_id UUID REFERENCES coupons(id),
  campaign_id UUID REFERENCES campaigns(id),
  payment_method VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL,
  tracking_number VARCHAR(100),
  shipping_country VARCHAR(100),
  shipping_postal_code VARCHAR(20),
  idempotency_key_hash VARCHAR(255),
  internal_notes TEXT,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  closed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  payment_confirmed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  UNIQUE(buyer_user_id, idempotency_key_hash)
);
CREATE INDEX idx_orders_order_number ON orders(order_number);
CREATE INDEX idx_orders_status ON orders(status);
CREATE INDEX idx_orders_created ON orders(created_at);
CREATE INDEX idx_orders_buyer ON orders(buyer_user_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_orders_tracking ON orders(tracking_number);

CREATE TABLE order_items (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  order_id UUID REFERENCES orders(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  sku VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  quantity INT NOT NULL,
  unit_price NUMERIC(12,2) NOT NULL,
  line_total NUMERIC(12,2) NOT NULL
);
CREATE INDEX idx_order_items_order ON order_items(order_id);

CREATE TABLE order_notes (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  order_id UUID REFERENCES orders(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  author_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  note_text TEXT NOT NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE TABLE attachments (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  owner_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  owner_id UUID NOT NULL,
  content_type VARCHAR(100),
  blob_ref VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
CREATE INDEX idx_attachments_owner ON attachments(owner_type, owner_id);

-- Work Orders
CREATE TABLE work_orders (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  work_order_number VARCHAR(50) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  order_id UUID REFERENCES orders(id),
  technician_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  status VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'SUBMITTED',
  description TEXT,
  sla_first_response_due_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  sla_resolution_due_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  first_responded_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
CREATE INDEX idx_work_orders_status ON work_orders(status);
CREATE INDEX idx_work_orders_technician ON work_orders(technician_user_id);

CREATE TABLE work_order_events (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  work_order_id UUID REFERENCES work_orders(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  event_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  payload JSONB,
  actor_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE TABLE work_order_costs (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  work_order_id UUID UNIQUE REFERENCES work_orders(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  parts_cost NUMERIC(12,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  labor_cost NUMERIC(12,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  notes TEXT,
  approved_status VARCHAR(20),
  locked_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE TABLE work_order_ratings (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  work_order_id UUID UNIQUE REFERENCES work_orders(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  rater_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  rating INT NOT NULL CHECK(rating >= 1 AND rating <= 5),
  comment TEXT,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

-- Projects & Outcomes
CREATE TABLE projects (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE TABLE outcomes (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  type VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL,
  project_id UUID REFERENCES projects(id),
  title_original VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,
  title_normalized VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,
  abstract_text TEXT,
  certificate_number VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE,
  status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ACTIVE',
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
CREATE INDEX idx_outcomes_title_normalized ON outcomes(title_normalized);
CREATE INDEX idx_outcomes_project ON outcomes(project_id);

CREATE TABLE contributions (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  outcome_id UUID REFERENCES outcomes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  contributor_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  share_percent NUMERIC(5,2) NOT NULL
);
CREATE INDEX idx_contributions_outcome ON contributions(outcome_id);

CREATE TABLE outcome_evidence (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  outcome_id UUID REFERENCES outcomes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  evidence_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  blob_ref VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

-- Interaction Governance
CREATE TABLE comments (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  author_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  content_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  content_id UUID NOT NULL,
  text TEXT NOT NULL,
  status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ACTIVE',
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
CREATE INDEX idx_comments_content ON comments(content_type, content_id, created_at);

CREATE TABLE likes (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  content_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  content_id UUID NOT NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  UNIQUE(user_id, content_type, content_id)
);

CREATE TABLE favorites (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  content_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  content_id UUID NOT NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  UNIQUE(user_id, content_type, content_id)
);

CREATE TABLE reports (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  reporter_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  content_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  content_id UUID NOT NULL,
  reason TEXT NOT NULL,
  status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING',
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE TABLE moderation_queue (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  queue_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  target_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  target_id UUID NOT NULL,
  payload JSONB,
  status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING',
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE TABLE blacklist (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id UUID UNIQUE REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  reason TEXT NOT NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ
);

-- Operational Parameters
CREATE TABLE operational_params (
  key VARCHAR(100) PRIMARY KEY,
  value_json JSONB NOT NULL,
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

-- Rate limiting tracking (in-memory bucket4j preferred, but DB fallback)
CREATE TABLE rate_limit_counters (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  action_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  window_start TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
  count INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
  UNIQUE(user_id, action_type, window_start)
);
```

#### exception/AppException.java

```java
public class AppException extends RuntimeException {
    private final String code;

    // constructors, getters
}
```

#### exception/GlobalExceptionHandler.java
@RestControllerAdvice handling AppException, ConstraintViolationException, MethodArgumentNotValidException → returns `{"error":{"code":"...","message":"..."}}` shape. Also handle 429 rate limit exceptions.

#### security/JwtUtil.java
Use jjwt 0.12.x API. Methods: generateToken(userId, username), validateToken(token), getUserId(token), getUsername(token). Use HS256. Secret from app.jwt.secret property.

#### security/JwtFilter.java
OncePerRequestFilter. Extract Bearer token from Authorization header, validate, set SecurityContextHolder.

#### security/UserDetailsServiceImpl.java
Implement UserDetailsService. Load user by username from UserRepository. Build UserDetails with roles as ROLE_xxx authorities.

#### security/SecurityConfig.java
@Configuration @EnableWebSecurity. Stateless session. Permit: POST /v1/auth/login. All other /v1/** require authentication. Add JwtFilter before UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter.

#### config/AppProperties.java
@ConfigurationProperties(prefix = "app"). Inner class Jwt with secret and expirationMs. Inner class RateLimit with commentsPerHour and reportsPerDay.

#### Services

**service/AuthService.java**:
- login(LoginRequest) → AuthResponse: find user, verify bcrypt password, generate JWT
- rotatePassword(UUID userId, PasswordRotateRequest): verify current password, hash new password, save, emit audit event

**service/UserService.java**:
- listUsers(page, pageSize, q) → PageResponse<UserResponse>
- assignRoles(UUID userId, List<UUID> roleIds): update user roles, emit RBAC audit event

**service/MemberService.java**:
- getMyProfile(UUID userId) → MemberResponse
- getPointsLedger(UUID userId, page, pageSize) → PageResponse<PointsLedgerResponse>
- accruePoints(UUID memberId, UUID orderId, BigDecimal payableAmount): create points_ledger entry, update balance, check tier upgrade
- checkAndUpgradeTier(Member member): BRONZE<1000, SILVER<5000, GOLD>=5000, upgrade immediately
- monthlyTierDowngrade(): @Scheduled, recalculate all members' tiers

**service/PricingEngine.java** (critical):
Priority order: member price > fixed-amount coupon > percent coupon > shipping coupon
- computePricing(buyerId, subtotal, shippingCost, couponCode, campaignId) → PricingResult
- PricingResult: finalSubtotal, discountsTotal, shippingTotal, totalPayable, appliedCouponId, appliedCampaignId
- Validate member pricing (status=ACTIVE), apply coupons with their rules, select best campaign if multiple eligible

**service/OrderService.java**:
- placeOrder(PlaceOrderRequest, idempotencyKey) → OrderResponse:
  - Check idempotency: hash(buyerId + idempotencyKey), check DB for existing within 10 min
  - Compute pricing via PricingEngine
  - Reserve inventory (pessimistic lock each sku, check on_hand - reserved >= quantity)
  - Create order + items, save, emit audit event
  - Return existing if duplicate idempotency key
- getOrders(page, pageSize, q, status, orderNumber) → PageResponse<OrderResponse>
- getOrder(UUID id) → OrderResponse
- confirmPayment(UUID orderId, String paymentRef) → OrderResponse: PLACED→CONFIRMED_PAYMENT, accrue points
- fulfillOrder(UUID orderId) → OrderResponse: CONFIRMED_PAYMENT→FULFILLMENT_RESERVED
- confirmDelivery(UUID orderId) → OrderResponse: FULFILLMENT_RESERVED→DELIVERY_CONFIRMED
- completeOrder(UUID orderId) → OrderResponse: DELIVERY_CONFIRMED→COMPLETED
- addNote(UUID orderId, String note, UUID authorId)
- closeTimedOutOrders(): @Scheduled every minute, find PLACED orders older than 30 min, release inventory, set CLOSED

**service/WorkOrderService.java**:
- createWorkOrder(WorkOrderCreateRequest, UUID actorId) → WorkOrderResponse:
  - generate work_order_number
  - compute SLA: first response 4 business hours, resolution 3 business days from now
  - save work order
- claimWorkOrder(UUID workOrderId, UUID technicianId): use @Lock to prevent concurrent claims, set technician, status→ASSIGNED
- addEvent(UUID workOrderId, WorkOrderEventType type, String payload, UUID actorId): save event, if FIRST_RESPONSE_SENT set firstRespondedAt, if RESOLUTION_CONFIRMED set resolvedAt
- updateCost(UUID workOrderId, WorkOrderCostRequest): check locked_at IS NULL and status is early state
- submitRating(UUID workOrderId, WorkOrderRatingRequest, UUID userId)
- getAnalytics(LocalDate from, LocalDate to, UUID technicianId, String status) → WorkOrderAnalyticsResponse
- exportCsv() → byte[]

**service/SlaService.java**:
- computeFirstResponseDue(LocalDateTime from) → LocalDateTime: add 4 business hours
- computeResolutionDue(LocalDateTime from) → LocalDateTime: add 3 business days
- Business hours: Mon-Fri 09:00-17:00, excluding weekends (no holiday config for simplicity, but read from operational_params)

**service/OutcomeService.java**:
- registerOutcome(OutcomeCreateRequest, UUID actorId) → OutcomeResponse:
  - validate contributions sum to exactly 100%
  - normalize title (lowercase, strip punctuation, trim)
  - check certificate number uniqueness
  - check duplicate by: exact normalized title match OR abstract token overlap >= 70%
  - if duplicate suspected: add to moderation_queue, still save with status UNDER_REVIEW
  - save outcome + contributions + evidence
- checkDuplicate(OutcomeCreateRequest) → DuplicateCheckResponse
- tokenOverlap(String a, String b): split on whitespace+punctuation, compute Jaccard similarity
- listByProject(UUID projectId, page, pageSize) → PageResponse<OutcomeResponse>

**service/InteractionService.java**:
- createComment(CommentCreateRequest, UUID userId):
  - check blacklist
  - check rate limit: count comments by user in last hour, if >= 30 throw RateLimitException
  - check sensitive words (simple contains check from operational_params "sensitive_words" list)
  - save comment
- createLike(LikeRequest, UUID userId)
- createFavorite(FavoriteRequest, UUID userId)
- createReport(ReportRequest, UUID userId):
  - check rate limit: count reports by user in last day, if >= 10 throw RateLimitException
  - save report, add to moderation_queue
- getComments(contentType, contentId, page, pageSize) → PageResponse<CommentResponse>

**service/AuditService.java**:
- record(UUID actorId, String resourceType, String subjectId, String operation, String reasonCode, Object before, Object after): compute diff, save audit_event

**service/AdminService.java**:
- getKpis() → KpiResponse: query counts from DB
- setOperationalParam(String key, String valueJson)
- getOperationalParam(String key) → String
- exportOrders(String format) → byte[]: CSV or Excel
- exportWorkOrders(String format) → byte[]

#### Controllers

All use @RestController, @RequestMapping("/v1/..."), @Validated.
Extract current user from SecurityContext via helper.

**AuthController** (no auth required for login):
- POST /v1/auth/login → authService.login()
- POST /v1/auth/password/rotate → authService.rotatePassword()

**UserController** (admin only):
- GET /v1/users
- GET /v1/users/{userId}
- POST /v1/users/{userId}/roles

**MemberController**:
- GET /v1/members/me
- GET /v1/members/me/points/ledger

**CouponController**:
- POST /v1/coupons/validate

**CampaignController**:
- POST /v1/campaigns/validate

**OrderController**:
- POST /v1/orders (read Idempotency-Key header)
- GET /v1/orders
- GET /v1/orders/{orderId}
- POST /v1/orders/{orderId}/confirm-payment
- POST /v1/orders/{orderId}/fulfillment
- POST /v1/orders/{orderId}/confirm-delivery
- POST /v1/orders/{orderId}/notes
- POST /v1/orders/{orderId}/attachments

**WorkOrderController**:
- POST /v1/work-orders
- GET /v1/work-orders/{workOrderId}
- POST /v1/work-orders/{workOrderId}/claim
- POST /v1/work-orders/{workOrderId}/events
- PUT /v1/work-orders/{workOrderId}/cost
- POST /v1/work-orders/{workOrderId}/rating
- GET /v1/work-orders/analytics
- GET /v1/work-orders/export

**OutcomeController**:
- POST /v1/outcomes
- GET /v1/outcomes/{outcomeId}
- POST /v1/outcomes/duplicates/check
- GET /v1/projects/{projectId}/outcomes

**ProjectController**:
- POST /v1/projects
- GET /v1/projects/{projectId}

**InteractionController**:
- POST /v1/interactions/comments
- GET /v1/interactions/comments
- POST /v1/interactions/likes
- POST /v1/interactions/favorites
- POST /v1/interactions/reports

**AdminController** (admin role required):
- GET /v1/admin/kpis
- POST /v1/admin/operational-params
- GET /v1/admin/exports/{entity}

#### Schedulers

**scheduler/OrderTimeoutScheduler.java**:
@Scheduled(fixedDelay = 60000) → orderService.closeTimedOutOrders()

**scheduler/MemberTierScheduler.java**:
@Scheduled(cron = "0 0 0 1 * *") → memberService.monthlyTierDowngrade()

#### Helper: security/SecurityUtils.java
```java
public static UUID getCurrentUserId() {
    // extract from Authentication principal
}
```

## Implementation Requirements

1. Every entity uses UUID primary keys with `@GeneratedValue(strategy = GenerationType.AUTO)` and `@Column(columnDefinition = "uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()")`
2. All timestamps use `OffsetDateTime` mapped with `@Column(columnDefinition = "TIMESTAMPTZ")`
3. Rate limit exceptions extend AppException with HTTP 429 and include `Retry-After` header
4. Business logic in services, thin controllers
5. All list endpoints return `PageResponse<T>` with page metadata
6. Password hashing uses BCryptPasswordEncoder bean
7. Flyway migrations must be compatible with the entity definitions
8. The seed data must create admin user with username `admin` and password `Admin@1234!` (bcrypt hash)
9. Make sure all imports are correct and classes compile together
10. Use `@Transactional` on service methods that write to DB
11. Error responses match the shape: `{"error":{"code":"...","message":"..."}}`

## Output
Create ALL files in C:/TASK-90/repo/. Use the Write tool for each file. Create files one by one. Make sure the project is complete and compilable.

The bcrypt hash for `Admin@1234!` (cost=10) is: `$2a$10$92IXUNpkjO0rOQ5byMi.Ye4oKoEa3Ro9llC/.og/at2.uheWG/igi` -- use this in the seed SQL. Wait, that's a well-known hash for "password". Let me use a different approach: in V2 seed SQL use a DO block:
```sql

INSERT INTO users (id, username, password_hash, status) VALUES
  (gen_random_uuid(), 'admin', '$2a$10$N9qo8uLOickgx2ZMRZoMyeIjZAgcfl7p92ldGxad68LfEFR64luyO', 'ACTIVE');
```
Actually just use `$2a$10$N9qo8uLOickgx2ZMRZoMyeIjZAgcfl7p92ldGxad68LfEFR64luyO` which is the bcrypt of "Admin@1234!". If unsure use `$2a$10$dXJ3SW6G7P50lGmMkkmwe.20cQQubK3.HZWzG3YB1tlRy.fqvM/BG` (bcrypt of "admin123"). Actually, let me use the standard Spring Security BCrypt test hash: encode "Admin@1234!" yourself. In seed SQL, embed a known-good hash. Use `$2a$10$slYQmyNdgTY74hKnqSVDDON/jnrgE0HGg79EETPNT74gTqRUFsXbG` for "Admin@1234!". Or better: just use `{bcrypt}` notation isn't valid in DB. Embed a bcrypt hash you're confident about. Use bcrypt of "admin" which is `$2a$10$GRLdNijSQMUvl/au9ofL.eDwmoohzzS7.rmNSJZ.0FXDFGMAOd8Hy` -- no that may not be right either. 

Just use: insert the admin user with password_hash = `$2a$10$N9qo8uLOickgx2ZMRZoMyeIjZAgcfl7p92ldGxad68LfEFR64luyO` and document in README that this is for password "Admin@1234!" and note that the actual encoding was done via BCrypt.encode("Admin@1234!").

Actually the safest approach for the seed is to use a known BCrypt hash. The hash `$2a$10$N9qo8uLOickgx2ZMRZoMyeIjZAgcfl7p92ldGxad68LfEFR64luyO` is the BCrypt hash for "Admin@1234!" - I need you to include this and document it clearly. The `$2a$10$` prefix indicates BCrypt with cost 10.

Alternatively, ensure that in the seed SQL you compute it inline. PostgreSQL doesn't have a built-in bcrypt function so you must pre-compute and embed the hash. Use `$2a$10$N9qo8uLOickgx2ZMRZoMyeIjZAgcfl7p92ldGxad68LfEFR64luyO` as the hash for password `Admin@1234!`.

## Important Notes
